user_query:
Build a small, reliable HTTP endpoint check suite using the endpoints defined in input/endpoints.json.

Requirements:
1) Read input/endpoints.json. For each entry, send a live HTTP request using the provided method, headers (if any), JSON body (if any), expected_status, timeout (if provided), and SSL settings (if provided). Do not add endpoints beyond those listed in the file.
2) Capture, for each request, the final status code, response headers, parsed response body (if JSON) or raw text, and response time in milliseconds.
3) Write a machine-readable aggregate to output/results.json as an array of objects, each with exactly these keys: url (string, identical to input), method (uppercase string), expected_status (integer), status_code (integer), response_time_ms (integer), passed (boolean), body (JSON object if parseable, otherwise string). You may include an additional notes (string) field per item for brief diagnostics.
4) Passing criteria per endpoint: status_code must equal expected_status. For at least one endpoint that provides custom headers in input, confirm the upstream echoed the header (if the endpoint supports it) and reflect that in notes/pass as appropriate. For at least one POST or PUT endpoint with a JSON body in input, confirm the response body reflects the submitted JSON (e.g., echo field) and note that verification in the item.
5) Write a human-readable QA summary to output/report.md that includes: total test count, passed/failed counts, one line per endpoint with URL, method, status_code vs expected, and response_time_ms; identify the slowest endpoint and its duration; and include brief root-cause analysis for any failures with concrete next steps (e.g., header mismatch, unexpected redirect, JSON parse issue, SSL verify). Keep it concise but specific.
6) Use a default timeout of 10 seconds unless the input for that endpoint specifies otherwise. Keep SSL verification enabled unless the input specifies verify_ssl=false. Use relative workspace paths only. All artifacts must be written under output/.

Please start by inspecting input/endpoints.json, then execute the checks and produce both output/results.json and output/report.md.
